title: Twenty-eight new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta July 13
summary: On July 13, YKHC reports twenty-eight new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta. title: Federal subsistence hunting permits for Wrangell-St. Elias National Park and Preserve available starting July 26
summary: Wrangell-St. Elias National Park and Preserve News Release Release Date: July 13, 2022 Contact: Carrie Wittmer, carrie_wittmer@nps.gov , 907-822-7255 COPPER CENTER, AK – Federal subsistence registration permits for hunts in Wrangell-St. Elias National Park and Preserve will be available starting Tuesday, July 26, at the park’s Visitor Center in Copper Center and the Slana Ranger Station. The Visitor Center is open seven days a week from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. through September 18. The Slana Ranger Station is open seven days a week from 9 a.m. to...

title: ConocoPhillips’ Willow Oil Development Proposal Threatens Landscapes, Communities and Our Climate
summary: Imagine a place about the size of Maine, with a breathtaking expanse of open tundra, countless lakes and sparkling ponds, meandering rivers and coastal lagoons, and the majestic Brooks Range rising in the distance. The western Arctic is a boundless wilderness that stretches our imagination to a time when buffalo freely roamed across the Great Plains. I’ve traveled this incredibly vast and beautiful region of Alaska known as the National Petroleum...

title: The AHAB Network Website has a New Look!

title: A New Airline Makes Alaska A Stopover Destination
summary: Northern Pacific Airways aims to make Alaska a stopover destination for flyers traveling between the United States and Asia. The post A New Airline Makes Alaska A Stopover Destination appeared first on Alaska Magazine .

title: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline is coming to Alaska
summary: Alaskans who dial 988 from the 907 area code will be connected with the Alaska Careline, a member of the Lifeline and an in-state call center where trained counselors answer calls, chats, and texts. The post 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline is coming to Alaska first appeared on Chugachmiut .
